loader.title: Loading portfolio system
loader.subtitle: Preparing a cleaner view of product delivery, case studies, and backend-backed content.

profile.name: Peter Ogba
profile.headline: I build product interfaces that read clearly and backend systems that hold up when the pressure is real.
profile.summary: Lagos-based engineer shipping React, TypeScript, Node.js, and production-minded delivery systems for teams that need sharp user experience and disciplined execution in the same product lane.
profile.availability: Open to freelance builds, contract delivery, and product teams that care about quality.

hero.panelTitle: Clear case studies, stronger product visuals, and backend-backed content.

services.title: Product work that stays sharp in the interface and stable in delivery.
services.copy: The objective is not decorative polish. It is stronger hierarchy, better contracts, and cleaner execution so the product feels deliberate from UI to runtime behavior.

projects.title: Selected projects with clear product context and delivery signals.
projects.copy: Each project card is meant to show the product type, the problem space, the technical lane, and the result instead of relying on vague portfolio language.

process.title: A build path that keeps the frontend readable and the system maintainable.
process.copy: The delivery model stays explicit: clarify the brief, shape the system, then ship a cleaner interface with a runtime path that can survive handoff and growth.

contact.title: Need a cleaner frontend, a steadier backend, or both.
contact.copy: Use the form for serious product work, redesigns, implementation support, or delivery cleanup. The request goes through the backend contact endpoint rather than disappearing into a static mailto shell.

projectShowcase.privateLabel: Private build. Walkthrough available on request.

contactForm.helperText: Serious briefs only. Requests are stored through the backend contact endpoint with project metadata so follow-up does not depend on a static inbox experience.

message.label: Project brief
message.placeholder: What are you building, what feels weak right now, and what outcome do you need from the rebuild?

footer.tagline: Readable interfaces, stable delivery, and product systems that keep working after launch.

heroMetrics.label: Production delivery
heroMetrics.detail: Shipping modern web and mobile-facing products with stronger execution discipline.

heroMetrics.label: Project cycles
heroMetrics.detail: Across launches, refactors, experiments, and client work.

heroMetrics.label: Core operating lane
heroMetrics.detail: Frontend precision paired with backend ownership.

servicePillars.title: Frontend Systems
servicePillars.description: Responsive interfaces with cleaner hierarchy, better visual pacing, and product-focused interaction design rather than cluttered components.
bullets: Design systems and reusable UI foundations

servicePillars.title: Backend Delivery
servicePillars.description: APIs, validation, runtime safeguards, and operational paths that turn a polished frontend into a dependable application.

servicePillars.title: Launch Discipline
servicePillars.description: I do not stop at UI polish. I wire the content, deployment path, and runtime behavior needed to ship cleanly and evolve safely.
bullets: Production build flow and environment handling

featuredProjects.title: Cashra Finance Manager
featuredProjects.summary: A finance manager designed around budget visibility, transaction clarity, and reporting that helps users act quickly.
featuredProjects.impact: The product flow was simplified around the actions people repeat most, so the dashboard reads faster and the reporting layer feels more useful.
highlights: Reduced noise in budgeting and reporting flows
Organized key actions around recurring money tasks
Kept the UI focused on visibility rather than dashboard decoration

featuredProjects.title: ClaudyGod Music & Ministries
featuredProjects.summary: A ministry and music platform that brings streaming, event visibility, and audience updates into one clearer discovery flow.
featuredProjects.impact: The browsing structure was cleaned up so users can move through music, media, and ministry content without visual overload.
featuredProjects.spotlight: Media discovery with a cleaner browsing path
highlights: Improved the browsing path for music, media, and updates
Balanced ministry communication with media-platform structure
Kept the experience accessible across desktop and mobile screens

featuredProjects.title: ClaudyGod Music App
featuredProjects.summary: A mobile listening experience built around music, playlists, messages, and release discovery for smaller screens.
featuredProjects.impact: The mobile product extends the platform beyond the browser with a more focused listening flow and better continuity for returning users.
featuredProjects.spotlight: Mobile-first access to streaming and playlists
highlights: Carried the platform into a dedicated mobile product lane
Focused the experience on listening, playlists, and continuity
Shaped the interface for smaller screens without losing clarity

processSteps.title: Clarify the brief
processSteps.description: Translate vague requirements into concrete screens, contracts, and delivery priorities before writing implementation code.

processSteps.title: Shape the system
processSteps.description: Define the content model, API boundaries, runtime safeguards, and component structure early so the work scales cleanly.

processSteps.title: Ship with intent
processSteps.description: Build the interface, wire the backend, and remove dead weight so the result feels coherent instead of patched together.

processSteps.title: Prepare for production
processSteps.description: Verify build paths, storage behavior, and deployment instructions so the work is ready to run, not just ready to demo.

systemSignals.label: content mode
systemSignals.detail: The primary site copy, navigation, case-study structure, and form options now come from a shared backend-served content contract.

systemSignals.label: contact flow
systemSignals.detail: Submissions move through the backend contact endpoint with richer request metadata instead of a hard-coded frontend-only mail flow.

systemSignals.label: ops posture
systemSignals.detail: Health checks, interaction tracking, and environment-aware site responses are included in the delivery path.
